notes.hypothesis: After wiping s3 to an air-dry state, placing only s3 into water should create a large rapid raw ADC drop on s3 while the other dry probes remain mostly unchanged.
notes.method: s3 was wiped/air-dried and then placed into the water cup during a 90 s capture at 0.5 s cadence. The other probes remained dry/static. Timing includes manual placement time.
notes.findings: s3 dropped from 2927 to 1004 raw ADC, a -1923 count response. The other channels stayed dry with smaller drift: s1 +26, s2 +12, s4 +35 counts.
notes.conclusion: The s3 dry-to-wet response was strong and fast. The result supports the same channel-local behavior seen on s4, with only small background dry drift on the other probes.
